req.title: Rate limiting (boundary variant)
req.statement: The API shall enforce 60 requests per minute per user; overages shall return 429 with retry-after. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-049 mandates a 60 req/min/user rate limit with 429+Retry-After on overage. Four tests are designed: (1) EP-positive: 59 requests within one minute all succeed with 2xx; (2) BVA-boundary-lower: exactly 60 requests succeed (the inclusive upper threshold of the allowed window); (3) BVA-boundary-upper: the 61st request triggers 429+Retry-After (one past the limit); (4) NEG-negative: a burst of 80 requests confirms the tail is consistently rejected. Each test resets state via a fresh authenticated user/token fixture and uses a monotonic clock to stay within a single 60-second window, ensuring determinism and idempotency.

tests.name: test_requests_below_limit_succeed
preconditions: A valid user token is provisioned via <fixture: fresh_user_token>
Rate-limit counter for the user is at zero (new token per test)
actions: Send 59 sequential GET /api/v1/accounts requests within one 60-second window
Record the HTTP status code of each response
expected: No response contains a Retry-After header
trace: R-FIN-049: requests below the 60 req/min threshold must not be rate-limited
EP partition: request count in [1, 59] -> allowed

tests.name: test_exactly_60_requests_succeed
preconditions: A valid user token is provisioned via <fixture: fresh_user_token>
Rate-limit counter for the user is at zero (new token per test)
actions: Send exactly 60 sequential GET /api/v1/accounts requests within one 60-second window
Record the HTTP status code of each response
expected: No response contains a Retry-After header
trace: R-FIN-049: boundary at exactly 60 requests must be permitted
BVA: test the inclusive upper boundary of the allowed partition (N=60)

tests.name: test_61st_request_returns_429_with_retry_after
preconditions: A valid user token is provisioned via <fixture: fresh_user_token>
Rate-limit counter for the user is at zero (new token per test)
actions: Send 60 sequential GET /api/v1/accounts requests within one 60-second window to exhaust the quota
Send a 61st GET /api/v1/accounts request in the same window
Inspect the status code and headers of the 61st response
expected: Response 61 contains a Retry-After header with a positive integer value
trace: R-FIN-049: the first request beyond the 60-per-minute limit must be rejected with 429
BVA: test N=61, one past the inclusive upper boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
import time
from <helper_fn> import make_authed_request

@pytest.fixture
def token(fresh_user_token):
    return fresh_user_token

def test_61st_request_returns_429_with_retry_after(token):
    start = time.monotonic()
    for i in range(60):
        r = make_authed_request("GET", "/api/v1/accounts", token=token)
        assert r.status_code in range(200, 300), (
            f"Setup request {i+1} expected 2xx, got {r.status_code}"
        )
    assert time.monotonic() - start < 60, "Quota exhaustion must stay in one window"
    r61 = make_authed_request("GET", "/api/v1/accounts", token=token)
    assert r61.status_code == 429, (
        f"Expected 429, got {r61.status_code}"
    )
    assert "Retry-After" in r61.headers, "429 response must include Retry-After header"
    retry_after = int(r61.headers["Retry-After"])
    assert retry_after > 0, "Retry-After must be a positive integer (seconds)"

tests.name: test_burst_beyond_limit_tail_rejected
preconditions: A valid user token is provisioned via <fixture: fresh_user_token>
Rate-limit counter for the user is at zero (new token per test)
actions: Send 80 rapid GET /api/v1/accounts requests within one 60-second window
Partition responses into those with 2xx and those with 429
Validate the count of each partition and the presence of Retry-After on all 429s
expected: Every 429 response carries a Retry-After header with a positive integer value
No 2xx response carries a Retry-After header
trace: R-FIN-049: requests beyond the limit (error pathway: request tail rejected) must be denied
NEG: deliberate overage to verify enforcement across the entire tail
tests.executable:
import pytest
import time
from <helper_fn> import make_authed_request

@pytest.fixture
def token(fresh_user_token):
    return fresh_user_token

def test_burst_beyond_limit_tail_rejected(token):
    TOTAL, LIMIT = 80, 60
    responses = []
    start = time.monotonic()
    for _ in range(TOTAL):
        r = make_authed_request("GET", "/api/v1/accounts", token=token)
        responses.append(r)
    assert time.monotonic() - start < 60, "All 80 requests must fall in one window"
    ok = [r for r in responses if r.status_code in range(200, 300)]
    rejected = [r for r in responses if r.status_code == 429]
    assert len(ok) == LIMIT, f"Expected {LIMIT} allowed, got {len(ok)}"
    assert len(rejected) == TOTAL - LIMIT, (
        f"Expected {TOTAL - LIMIT} rejections, got {len(rejected)}"
    )
    for r in rejected:
        assert "Retry-After" in r.headers
        assert int(r.headers["Retry-After"]) > 0
    for r in ok:
        assert "Retry-After" not in r.headers
